when_to_use: Hammond B-3 organ trio/quintet, gospel-blues 12-bar, organ solo with a Leslie/drawbar dynamic arc, 65-145 BPM

defaults.feel: bluesy swung 8ths, thick backbeat laid behind the grid, organ bass pedal legato

signature_moves: gospel plagal cadence: Bb/F -> F7 (IV/I -> I)
12-bar gospel-blues turnaround: Gm7 -> C7(#9) -> F7 D7 -> Gm7 C7
diminished passing chord: Bb7 -> Bbdim7 -> F7
cadence_options: gospel plagal: IV -> I, or IV/I -> I
outro hold: land on 7#9 or 6/9, sustained with an organ glissando (palm smear)
avoid: dense ii-V chains or bebop-level harmonic motion - soul jazz stays on 12-bar gospel-blues, deliberately sparse (see soul-jazz-genre.md SS5-6)
a tight, precise big-band ride feel - the backbeat should sit behind the grid, not on it (see soul-jazz-swing groove profile)

hook_archetypes.name: organ-vamp-pedal
hook_archetypes.rhythm: a repeating bass-pedal figure the organ states alone before the band enters
hook_archetypes.contour: root-5th pedal under a simple chordal comp, Leslie set slow (chorale)

hook_archetypes.name: call-and-response-riff
hook_archetypes.rhythm: a 2-bar horn riff answered by a 2-bar organ comp fill
hook_archetypes.contour: tenor sax/trumpet state a bluesy unison phrase, organ answers with a gospel-tinged chordal fill

head.contour: singable unison/harmony line over the 12-bar form, gospel-inflected
head.placement: laid slightly behind the grid, matching the backbeat

organ-solo.note_lengths: phrases lengthen and thicken as the solo builds
organ-solo.contour: drawbars pulled out gradually, runs get faster and denser toward the peak
organ-solo.placement: pushes forward at the peak as the Leslie switches to fast tremolo

sax-guitar-solo.note_lengths: shorter, bluesier phrases, call-and-response with organ comping
sax-guitar-solo.contour: blues/gospel scale based (SS7), sub-tone or thumb-picked depending on instrument

head-return.note_lengths: back to the head's clear phrasing
head-return.placement: laid slightly behind the grid, matching the backbeat

drum_skeleton.low_energy: ride swung 8ths, thick snare backbeat on 2 and 4 laid behind the grid, hi-hat mostly closed
drum_skeleton.mid_energy: hi-hat opens (sloshy) on off-beat accents, kick more present under the organ pedal
drum_skeleton.high_energy: ride pushes harder, snare accents thicken under the organ/sax solo peak, hi-hat sloshier

arrangement_defaults.layout_rules: organ states the intro vamp alone (Leslie slow/chorale); horns state the head with organ comping underneath; organ solo builds by pulling drawbars and switching Leslie to fast tremolo at the peak; sax/guitar solo trades the foreground while organ returns to comping; head returns with full band; outro holds a 7#9 or 6/9 chord with an upward organ glissando

anti_boredom_rules: the organ solo must have a dynamic arc - drawbars and Leslie speed change across the solo, it cannot stay one static registration the whole time
keep harmony on the sparse 12-bar gospel-blues vocabulary - do not drift into dense ii-V chains (see harmony_palette avoid)
the backbeat stays laid behind the grid at every energy level - if it tightens onto the grid the piece has drifted toward classic-jazz-swing
the call-and-response-riff hook must recur, varied by section or instrument, never copy-pasted identically
velocity is never flat across a voice within a bar (comping fills low, riff/solo peaks high)
